kill_fact: The spin is an undated, board-discretionary corporate intention, not a binding dated event, and the spread has already closed. Primary sources confirm the timeline slipped from 'late 2026' to 'early 2027' (June 23, 2026 8-K), there is NO record date and NO effective date and NO Form 10, and the separation is explicitly gated on 'appropriate leverage levels' and 'supportive market conditions' (a real discretionary out-clause). On the SAME June 23 release the designated Global Coffee Co. CEO (Rafa Oliveira) announced he is leaving end of July 2026 — forcing a fresh CEO search led by Chair Pamela Patsley weeks before the larger spin entity is meant to stand up. The HARD leg (the $18B JDE Peet's tender) already fired April 1, 2026, so there is no live deal-break arb spread to capture. With KDP at ~$33.4 (mid of its $24.88-$35.94 52-week range) vs a ~$37.7 SoP target and a ~$28.5 business floor, reward/risk is below 1:1 (~+13% upside / -14% downside). The bull's index-flow re-rating has no forcing function — nothing external compels the board to declare a date — and the $28.5 'floor' is a staples valuation anchor (~24x P/E, ~2.8% covered yield) atop ~4.5x leverage, NOT a cash/recovery backstop, so it can break in a multiple de-rate or coffee-drag miss. The asymmetry does not survive at this price: watch-list, not a dated special situation.
corrections: None material to the bear case — the dossier's load-bearing facts are all independently verified and correct: April 1 2026 JDE close (96.22% tendered at EUR 31.85/sh), separation slipped to undated 'early 2027' as of June 23 2026, Oliveira departure end of July 2026 with a fresh CEO search, ~4.5x post-deal leverage as the gating constraint, live price ~$33 mid-range, sub-1:1 reward/risk. Labeling nuance only: the $28.5 'floor' should be described as a valuation anchor on a levered staples business, NOT a 'verified downside floor' implying a hard recovery/cash backstop.
evidence: SEC Form 8-K (CIK 0001418135), exhibit eh260797101_ex9901, dated June 23, 2026 — leadership updates ahead of separation; separation targeted for 'early 2027'; Rafa Oliveira (Coffee Operating Unit head / designated Global Coffee Co. CEO) to depart end of July 2026; Chair Pamela Patsley to lead the Global Coffee Co. CEO search and chair its board; Tim Cofer to oversee coffee in the interim and become Beverage Co. CEO post-separation.

SEC Form 8-K (CIK 1418135) exhibit d137180dex991 + Econic Partners deal note — April 1, 2026 close of the ~$18B JDE Peet's acquisition; Kodiak BidCo paid EUR 31.85/share for shares tendered (96.22%); financing ~$9B new long-term debt + ~$8.5B equity (incl. ~$4.5B Apollo/KKR/T. Rowe convertible preferred) + ~$5B assumed JDE bonds; combined net leverage ~4.5x.

Yahoo Finance / CNBC quote, June 26, 2026 — KDP ~$33.39-$33.40, intraday $32.36-$33.33, 52-week range $24.88-$35.94, P/E ~24, yield ~2.83%; Barclays Overweight $36 target; consensus 12-mo target ~$33.25 (range $28-$42) — i.e. the spin is already priced as a coin-flip, spread effectively closed.

KDP Feb 23, 2026 financing-plan release (news.keurigdrpepper.com) + SEC 8-K/A pro-forma (kdp-ex992) — original workstreams targeted only 'operational readiness to separate by year-end 2026' (since slipped), and separation remains gated on deleveraging/market conditions rather than any fixed record or effective date.
